title: heuchera micrantha var. glaberrima
intro: Heuchera micrantha var. glaberrima, also known as Alum root, is a perennial plant that belongs to the saxifrage family and is native to North America. It has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are attributed to the presence of salicylic acid, a compound similar to aspirin. The health benefits of Alum root include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and treat skin conditions such as eczema and acne. Alum root can be prepared for medicinal use through infusions, decoctions, or salves, and it is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its therapeutic effects.
